_note: Per-lesson role-play scenario. Grounded in A2/010 dialogue + vocab. Learner plays Tom; computer plays Anna. Each NPC node offers 2-3 branches so the same lesson language can be reached different ways. Consumed by RP.Engine + RP.classifiers.local (see roleplay/).
title: 010 · A Visit to Grandma

npc.text: Last weekend I visited my grandma.

learning_objective: Use: nice, what did you do

routes.meaning: Tom says that is nice and asks what she did.
examples: That is nice! What did you do?
Nice! What did you do there?
That's nice! What did you do?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “That is nice! What did you do?”
You can ask “That is nice! What did you do?”

partial.message: Ask about her visit: “That is nice! What did you do?”

off_topic.message: Anna visited grandma. Say “That is nice! What did you do?”

npc.text: She lives near the beach. I love her house! We did fun things.

examples: That is nice! What did you do?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “That is nice! What did you do?”

routes.meaning: Tom says yum and asks if the cookies were good.

routes.hint: You can react, or ask “Yum! Were they good?”

partial.message: Ask about the cookies: “Yum! Were they good?”

off_topic.message: They baked cookies. Say “Yum! Were they good?”

npc.text: Me too! We made chocolate ones.

routes.meaning: Tom asks if the cookies were good.
Tom asks what else Anna did.
examples: And what else did you do?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “What else did you do?”

routes.meaning: Tom reacts to the delicious cookies.
examples: I wish I could try one.
routes.hint: You can react, or ask “What else did you do?”

partial.message: Ask for more: “What else did you do?”

off_topic.message: The cookies were great. Ask “What else did you do?”

npc.text: We watched old photos and she told me stories.

routes.meaning: Tom asks if Anna will visit grandma again.
examples: Are you going to visit her again?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Will you visit her again?”
You can ask more, or ask “Will you visit her again?”

partial.message: Ask about next time: “Will you visit her again?”

off_topic.message: Anna loves grandma. Ask “Will you visit her again?”

npc.text: Stories about when she was young. They were so fun!

routes.meaning: Tom asks if Anna will visit again.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Will you visit her again?”

off_topic.message: Ask Anna “Will you visit her again?”

npc.text: Yes, I will visit her next month.

routes.meaning: Tom asks Anna to say hello to grandma.
examples: Say hello to her for me!
Say hi to her for me!
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Say hello to her for me!”
You can ask to join, or say “Say hello to her for me!”

partial.message: Send greetings: “Say hello to her for me!”

off_topic.message: Anna visits next month. Say “Say hello to her for me!”

npc.text: Of course! Grandma would love to meet you.
I will! She will be happy. Thanks, Tom!
